page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the c3 family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 64 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It has no local storage or bundled GPUs, and includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data positions this instance primarily in the average tier across most compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in vector and matrix calculations. It achieved a single-core Geekbench score of 1706.0 and a multi-core score of 6586.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 544.59 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 51.18 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

While the instance demonstrates stable average performance for tasks like Redis SET operations at 8.86 million operations per second and static web serving at 13.12 GB/sec, certain multi-threaded and complex workloads show performance limitations. Specifically, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and text generation for medium 7B LLM models are classified in the weak tier, with the latter yielding 7.34 tokens per second. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and moderate-scale data processing that do not require local storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The hardware configuration delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, supported by advanced CPU instructions like AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmark results indicate average performance across most metrics, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 6586.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 8.86 million operations per second. However, performance tradeoffs are present, with weak results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM text generation. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and caching workloads that benefit from dedicated compute resources and high memory density without requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8481C CPU. It offers balanced memory bandwidth but shows weak multi-threaded decompression.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8481C processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory density for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM providing a high 1:8 resource ratio
Intel Xeon 8481C processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction support
Average performance in database operations and Redis caching benchmarks
Tradeoffs in multi-threaded decompression and medium LLM text generation
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for memory-intensive workloads and database applications
